# Claude Skills & Apps

> A curated, open-source collection of production-ready skills and apps for Claude Code, Cowork, and compatible AI coding agents. MIT license. Contributions welcome.

Repository: https://github.com/smartrus/claude-skills-and-apps

## What this repo is

This repository provides two types of artifacts:

- **Skills** — self-contained prompt modules (`SKILL.md` + optional scripts/references/assets) that give Claude specialized expertise in a domain. Drop a skill folder into your Claude Code project or Cowork session to activate it.
- **Apps** — standalone tools, dashboards, and automations built with Claude. Each app is fully documented and ready to run or adapt.

Skills are organized by domain under `skills/<domain>/<skill-name>/`. Apps are organized by category under `apps/<category>/<app-name>/`.

## Skill domains

- `skills/engineering/` — API migration, mutation testing, code review, architecture, debugging
- `skills/marketing/` — AI citation strategy, carousel content, cross-cultural UX auditing, immersive storytelling, semantic communications
- `skills/operations/` — AIOps telemetry, autonomous rollback, chaos engineering, cloud FinOps, game-day simulation, IaC drift remediation, Kubernetes debugging, multi-cloud governance, performance bottleneck analysis
- `skills/product/` — Accessibility (WCAG), cognitive load balancing, digital twin planning, human-AI trust architecture
- `skills/data/` — Agentic RAG evaluation, data poisoning auditing, semantic schema mapping
- `skills/design/` — Digital twin dashboards, inclusive visuals, whimsy injection, XR spatial interface design

## App categories

- `apps/tools/` — CLI utilities and interactive tools (e.g., `health-coach`: a personal health tracking agent)
- `apps/dashboards/` — Interactive HTML/React dashboards and reports
- `apps/automations/` — Scheduled tasks, workflows, and integrations

## Skill file format

Every skill has a `SKILL.md` with required YAML frontmatter:

```yaml
---
name: Skill Name
description: One-line description
version: "1.0.0"
author: Author Name
tags: [domain, category, keywords]
triggers: [phrases, that, activate, this, skill]
tools_required: [Read, Write, Bash]  # optional
---
```

The body of `SKILL.md` contains the full prompt/instructions Claude will follow when the skill is active.

## Key files

- [README.md](README.md) — overview, quick start, skill/app structure
- [CONTRIBUTING.md](CONTRIBUTING.md) — branch model, quality standards, PR process
- [skills/_template/SKILL.md](skills/_template/SKILL.md) — canonical template for new skills
- [apps/_template/README.md](apps/_template/README.md) — canonical template for new apps
- [scripts/new-skill.sh](scripts/new-skill.sh) — scaffolds a new skill
- [scripts/new-app.sh](scripts/new-app.sh) — scaffolds a new app
- [scripts/lint.sh](scripts/lint.sh) — validates frontmatter and structure before submission
- [scripts/validate-frontmatter.py](scripts/validate-frontmatter.py) — Python frontmatter validator
- [scripts/generate-catalog.py](scripts/generate-catalog.py) — generates the skill/app catalog

## Branch model

- `dev` — active development; all PRs must target this branch
- `main` — stable releases only; periodically merged from `dev`

PRs targeting `main` are automatically closed by CI.

## CI / quality gates

Six GitHub Actions workflows run on every PR:

1. `ci-quality-gate.yml` — full quality gate (frontmatter, lint, broken links)
2. `lint-skills.yml` — YAML frontmatter linting for skills
3. `security-audit.yml` — secret scanning, credential detection
4. `enforce-pr-target.yml` — rejects PRs targeting `main`
5. `claude-review.yml` — automated Claude code review
6. `docs-deploy.yml` — deploys MkDocs site on merge to `main`

## How to use a skill

Copy any skill folder into your Claude Code project:

```bash
cp -r skills/engineering/api-version-migrator ~/.claude/commands/
```

Or reference it directly in a Cowork session.

## How to contribute

```bash
git clone https://github.com/smartrus/claude-skills-and-apps.git
cd claude-skills-and-apps
git checkout dev
git checkout -b feat/my-skill
./scripts/new-skill.sh <domain> <skill-name>
# edit SKILL.md, then:
./scripts/lint.sh
git push -u origin feat/my-skill
# open PR targeting dev
```

Requirements: valid YAML frontmatter, no placeholders, no secrets, lint must pass.
